analysis.note: Predicate qualifier of vijñānam; the bhāṣya identifies this vijñāna as ālayavijñāna.

verification.detail: compound stem sarvabīja as Napumsaka/Prathama/Eka: pass; relation bahuvrihi: unsupported (logged)

analysis.note: Nominative neuter; contextually ālayavijñāna according to Sthiramati, not vijñāna in general.
Subject of yāti; defined by the bhāṣya as alteration from a previous state, with ālayavijñāna as the relevant basis.
First member of the distributive repetition tathā tathā, glossed by the bhāṣya with tasya tasya vikalpasya.
Second member of the distributive repetition; “such-and-such,” i.e. suited to each respective vikalpa.
Glossed by prāpnoti in the bhāṣya: “reaches/comes into.”
Causal ablative: “because of / through mutual influence or dependence.”

verification.detail: compound stem anyonyavaśa as Pum/Panchami/Eka: pass; relation tatpurusa: unsupported (logged)

analysis.note: Instrumental relative; morphologically also compatible with neuter, but here analyzed with pariṇāmaḥ as antecedent and rendered functionally as “whereby / by reason of which.”
Subject of jāyate; the bhāṣya characterizes it as anekaprakāra, “of manifold kinds.”
First member of distributive sa sa: “this or that / each respective.”
Second member of distributive sa sa.
Intransitive middle present in sense: “arises, is born”; the bhāṣya reinforces this with utpattir bhavati.

justifications.decision: Reference of sarvabījaṃ vijñānam and the compound sense of sarvabīja.
options_considered: a non-conscious pradhāna or similar principle is the all-seed
ālayavijñāna is all-seeded through possessing all dharma-producing powers
justifications.chosen: “Consciousness” here means ālayavijñāna, qualified as “all-seeded” because it carries the powers/seeds for producing all dharmas.

evidence.quote: tatra sarvadharmotpādanaśaktyanugamāt sarvabījaṃ vijñānam ity ālayavijñānam
evidence.translation: Here, because it is accompanied by the power to produce all dharmas, ‘all-seeded consciousness’ means ālayavijñāna.

evidence.quote:
vijñānaṃ hy asarvabījam apy astīty ataḥ sarvabījam ity āha
vijñānād anyad
api kaiścit pradhānādi sarvabījaṃ kalpyata iti vijñānam ity āha
evidence.translation: Since there is also consciousness that is not all-seeded, he says ‘all-seeded’; and since some imagine something other than consciousness, such as pradhāna, to be all-seeded, he says ‘consciousness.’

evidence.quote:
atha vā
ekapadavyabhicāre 'pi viśeṣaṇaviśeṣyatvadarśanān nāyaṃ doṣaḥ
evidence.translation: Alternatively, even if a single word is non-exclusive, this is no fault, because a qualifier-qualified relation is observed.

justifications.decision: Meaning of pariṇāma, force of yāti, and scope of tathā tathā.
options_considered: pariṇāma as an unspecified transformation merely occurring in various ways
pariṇāma as a separate substance or product
pariṇāma as a change of state reaching the immediate productive condition for each vikalpa
justifications.chosen: pariṇāma is “alteration from a prior state,” and tathā tathā yāti means that this transformation reaches the state capable of immediately producing each respective vikalpa.

evidence.quote:
pariṇāmas tathā tathā yāty anyonyavaśād iti
pūrvāvasthāto 'nyathābhāvaḥ pariṇāmaḥ
tathā tatheti tasya tasya
vikalpasyānantarotpādanasamarthāvasthāṃ prāpnotīty arthaḥ
evidence.translation: On ‘the transformation goes thus and thus because of mutual influence’: transformation is becoming otherwise than the previous state. ‘Thus and thus’ means ‘of each respective vikalpa’; the meaning is that it reaches a state capable of immediately producing that vikalpa.

options_considered: mutual influence among the vikalpas themselves
reciprocal conditionality between pravṛtti-vijñāna and ālayavijñāna-pariṇāma
justifications.chosen: anyonyavaśāt is reciprocal conditioning: eye-etc. consciousness and the ālaya-consciousness transformation are each a condition (nimitta) for the other.

evidence.quote:
anyonyavaśād iti
tathā hi cakṣurādivijñānaṃ svaśaktiparipoṣe vartamāne
śaktiviśiṣṭasyālayavijñānapariṇāmasya nimittaṃ so 'pi ālayavijñānapariṇāmaś
cakṣurādivijñānasya nimittaṃ bhavati
evam anyonyavaśād yasmād ubhayaṃ
pravartate tasmād ālayavijñānād anyenānadhiṣṭhitād anekaprakāro vikalpaḥ sa sa
evidence.translation: On ‘because of mutual influence’: when the nourishment of its own power is present, eye-etc. consciousness is a condition for the power-qualified transformation of ālayavijñāna; that ālayavijñāna-transformation too becomes a condition for eye-etc. consciousness. Thus, because both proceed through mutual influence, from that ālayavijñāna ...

justifications.decision: Construal of yena, sa sa, vikalpaḥ, and the voice/sense of jāyate.
options_considered: yena as introducing an external instrument or agent
sa sa as mere emphasis without distributive force
jāyate as a passive ‘is produced’ by an unstated external maker
yena as ‘whereby,’ with sa sa marking manifold respective vikalpas arising from ālayavijñāna
justifications.chosen: yena is rendered as correlative ‘whereby / by reason of which’; sa sa vikalpaḥ is distributive, ‘this or that construction,’ manifold and arising from ālayavijñāna without another governing basis. jāyate is therefore translated intransitively as ‘arises.’

evidence.quote:
evam anyonyavaśād yasmād ubhayaṃ
pravartate tasmād ālayavijñānād anyenānadhiṣṭhitād anekaprakāro vikalpaḥ sa sa
jāyate
evidence.translation: Thus, since both proceed through mutual influence, from that ālayavijñāna, not presided over by another, a manifold vikalpa, this or that, arises.

evidence.quote:
tatra ca vartamāne janmani yathālayavijñānāt
pravṛttivijñānasyotpattir bhavati tathākhyātam
evidence.translation: And there, with respect to the present life, how the arising of active consciousness occurs from ālayavijñāna has thus been explained.

translation:
For consciousness—store-consciousness (ālayavijñāna)—is all-seeded (sarvabīja):
by mutual influence its transformation (pariṇāma)
reaches such-and-such states,
and thereby this or that construction (vikalpa) arises.
one_shot_delta: A commentary-blind translation might take vijñāna as consciousness in general; Sthiramati explicitly identifies it as ālayavijñāna and explains why both sarvabīja and vijñāna are needed.
It might render tathā tathā vaguely as “in various ways”; the bhāṣya specifies: the transformation reaches states capable of immediately producing each respective vikalpa.

It might take anyonyavaśāt as vague mutual control or mutual influence among concepts; the commentary makes it the reciprocal nimitta-relation between cakṣurādi/prवṛtti-vijñāna and ālayavijñānapariṇāma.
It might posit another governing basis or producer for vikalpa; Sthiramati says vikalpa arises from ālayavijñāna, anyenānadhiṣṭhitāt, “not presided over by another.”
analyzer_disagreements: ByT5 segments sarva_bījam; I analyze sarvabījam as one inflected compound stem sarvabīja, because the bhāṣya treats sarvabījaṃ vijñānam as a qualifier-qualified expression identifying ālayavijñāna (trbh 1136–1137, 1141).

ByT5 segments anyonya_vaśāt; I analyze anyonyavaśāt as one inflected tatpuruṣa compound stem anyonyavaśa in the causal ablative, because the bhāṣya expounds anyonyavaśāt as reciprocal causal conditioning (nimitta) between cakṣurādivijñāna and ālayavijñānapariṇāma (trbh 1146–1150).
